abstract: A protocol to provide enumerations of all contact items and changed contact items.
overview:
## Overview

Implement this protocol to fetch contact items from your data store in a consistent, predictable order when the system requests contact item enumeration. The [doc://com.apple.ContactProvider/documentation/ContactProvider/ContactItemEnumerating] protocol, typically implemented by the app extension, returns an instance of this type when the system requests contacts.

The enumerator has two main methods to implement:

The following `RootContainerEnumerator` outlines how to implement a `ContactItemEnumerator`. The listing shows `TODO` in places where the implementation depends on the specifics of your app’s data store.

items.description: Enumerates all items, batched in pages.
items.name: enumerateContent(in:for:)

items.description: A fixed offset into enumerating all contact items.
items.name: ContactItemPage

items.description: A protocol that defines a system observer that receives a resumable enumeration of all items.
items.name: ContactItemContentObserver

items.description: Enumerates items changed since the last sync.
items.name: enumerateChanges(startingAt:for:)

items.description: A snapshot point into enumerating changed contact items.
items.name: ContactItemSyncAnchor

items.description: A protocol that defines a system observer that receives a resumable enumeration of changed contact items.
items.name: ContactItemChangeObserver

items.description: An item in the contact database.
items.name: ContactItem

items.description: A protocol to provide enumerators for collections of contact items.
items.name: ContactItemEnumerating

title: ContactItemEnumerator
